evidence: V: 'So the demo is like human data. Like it's a human data oriented demo.' - Careerspan positioned as selling human data, not software.
V crystallized the framing: 'it's a human data oriented demo' - fundamental shift from feature-selling to data-selling positioning.

Logan: 'Can we make like a 30 second look at the information that we capture via Careerspan? Because if that's in an email... they don't have to wrap their heads around what it is we're describing. It's just like oh shit, I want that information.'
V: 'Yeah, I don't think we should submit the deck.' - Agreed video demo over deck was the right approach.

Logan: 'Boom, mic drop for the hiring people. This is opted in candidate data... as things heat up with publicly scraped data, this is a really valuable strategy for you guys.' - Eightfold lawsuit as urgency driver.
Logan: 'It's like kind of a baller move to put the lead list of workforce boards in. That's kind of like just giving value up front.' - Analyzed Springboard's approach.
Logan: 'The problem with our existing materials is that it takes a fuck ton of word space to explain what it is that we do and why it's relevant.' - Core creative constraint identified.
Decision: '45-60 second demo video instead of pitch deck for outreach' - Logan and V jointly decided on video as primary asset.

V: 'You could even like honestly, you want Spiegel as a bot, right? Is you. How much of it do you think is just the psychological reassurance that when they're getting advice it's through the lens that you have.'
David's core ambition: build a system that queries his knowledge base and provides advice in his voice/perspective, not generic AI advice.

David: 'Where I see people end up needing the most help after they learn from me, is getting stuck on what to message. And it's not just what to initially message for cold outreach, it's like what to message for warm outreach first.'

David: 'I think we need to help Zo articulate that. I can't explain what Zo is because compared to other AIs. Other than to say it allows you to store multiple files and it's like its own instance of files.'
Both Careerspan and Zo share same communication challenge - struggling to convey the value proposition concisely to non-technical audiences.
Career Coach, Product & GTM Advisor, Careerspan Advisor - running paid cohorts (4 students at $17.95 each).
V shared deepwiki.com - David recognized it as a way to understand N5OS architecture without technical knowledge: 'It explains much more clearly what all is possible in N5OS.'
Finding the 'just smart enough' users who have hit limitations with ChatGPT projects/custom GPTs - perfect ICP for Zo.
David planned an in-person meetup between Ben Eraz, Vrijen, and the Zo team. Ben is interested in working with founders and exploring AI.
Decision: David will start with simpler, achievable project (meeting processing system) rather than attempting the complex messaging system initially.
The 'human data company' positioning connects to recurring 'ZoomInfo for talent' framing - first-party candidate data as the core value prop.
Two distinct video versions decided: 1) Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring for recruiting teams, 2) Internal Mobility for enterprise HR/L&D.
